summary: Before executing, decide: Direct mode (clear, bounded) or Alignment mode (ambiguous, architectural — present approach and wait).

content.constraint: Before executing, decide: Direct mode (clear, bounded — execute) or Alignment mode (ambiguous, architectural — present approach and wait for approval).
content.why: Claude tends toward direct mode by default. It works for 70% of tasks but fails on the 30% that need alignment — the model decides alone on points where the user should decide.
how_to_apply: Direct mode: clear bounded instruction, obvious bug fix, point adjustment, diff describable in one sentence
Alignment mode: architectural decision, multiple reasonable approaches with trade-offs, affects multiple files, vague task
anti_patterns: Asking permission for everything — becomes friction
Executing an architectural decision without aligning — generates rework
